subject: English / First Flight
chapter_name: Poems – How to Tell Wild Animals, The Ball Poem
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyze the themes, tone, and literary devices of both poems
Interpret humour, exaggeration, observation, and reflection at a deeper level
Connect literary techniques to thematic meaning and author’s intent
Develop analytical writing, discussion, and creative expression skills
Compare and contrast lessons about human experience, nature, and responsibility

lesson_plan.title: In-depth Introduction to Poets and Poetic Styles
topics_covered: Authors: Carolyn Wells and John Berryman – style, era, and influence
Overview of both poems with critical focus
Discuss literary tone: humour vs reflective depth
teaching_strategy: Provide contextual background on poets and era
Explain style, purpose, and target audience for each poem
Introduce analytical approach: observing tone, devices, and theme
student_activities: Annotate first impressions of humour and reflection in the poems
Discuss in pairs how style affects meaning
assessment: Class discussion on poet’s style and intent
Check annotated notes for insight and depth

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Analysis – How to Tell Wild Animals
topics_covered: Examine literary devices: exaggeration, rhyme, rhythm, imagery
Explore the role of humour and instruction in teaching observational skills
Analyse the narrative voice and tone
teaching_strategy: Break down key stanzas for literary device analysis
Compare exaggerated animal behaviour to real traits
Lead discussion on how humour conveys learning
student_activities: Identify examples of exaggeration, rhyme, and imagery
Explain how these devices reinforce the poem’s theme
Write a short analytical note on tone and style
assessment: Check analytical notes for critical understanding

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Analysis – The Ball Poem
topics_covered: Examine themes of loss, responsibility, and personal growth
Analyse imagery, repetition, and reflective language
Discuss universal lessons and emotional resonance
teaching_strategy: Explain metaphors and symbolism: ball as possessions/loss
Highlight repetition and imagery emphasizing grief and learning
Encourage students to connect poem to human experiences
student_activities: Identify and annotate metaphors, imagery, and repetition
Explain how literary devices deepen emotional impact
Summarize how the boy learns responsibility
assessment: Evaluate written summaries for depth of analysis

lesson_plan.title: Comparative Critical Study
topics_covered: Compare humour vs reflection, exaggeration vs emotional depth
Discuss authorial intent and audience engagement
Analyse how both poems teach lessons through different approaches
teaching_strategy: Create a comparative chart of themes, devices, tone, and lessons
Guide students to identify contrasts and similarities in style and purpose
student_activities: Complete the comparative chart individually or in groups
Discuss which literary devices are most effective in each poem
assessment: Evaluate chart for completeness and critical insight

lesson_plan.title: Creative and Analytical Expression
topics_covered: Apply critical understanding to writing and visualization
Encourage analytical and reflective creative work
teaching_strategy: Assign creative writing from a perspective in the poems
Encourage visualization of themes with annotations and symbolic drawings
student_activities: Write a 150–200 word analytical paragraph on humour, reflection, and lessons in both poems
Draw an illustration showing an animal with exaggerated traits or the boy learning responsibility
Present findings and interpretations to the class
assessment: Evaluate depth of analysis and creativity in writing and drawing
Assess clarity of reasoning and connection to poems

teaching_aids: Printed or projected texts of both poems with annotations
Images of animals and playground objects for illustration
Charts comparing literary devices, tone, and themes
Flashcards with examples of humour, exaggeration, imagery, and reflection
evaluation_methods: Oral questioning and discussion on literary analysis
teacher_tip: Encourage students to critically analyze literary devices, tone, and thematic depth. Facilitate discussion connecting humour, observation, and reflection to life lessons. Promote synthesis of understanding through creative and analytical expression.
